title: Calendar group filtering: filter which groups appear in the calendar view
body:
## Issue description

When a user views their calendar, it aggregates personal events and events shared to all groups they belong to. As users join more groups, the calendar can become cluttered. Users need the ability to filter which groups' shared events appear in the calendar view so they can focus on the events most relevant to them.

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

### 1. Group filter UI component

- Display a filter panel (sidebar, dropdown, or toggle list) showing all groups the user belongs to
- Each group has a toggle (checkbox or switch) to show/hide its shared events in the calendar
- Include a "Personal events" toggle to show/hide the user's own personal (unshared) events
- Include "Select all" / "Deselect all" convenience controls
- Each group entry shows:
  - Group name
  - Color swatch (for distinguishing events from different groups in the calendar)
  - Toggle state (on/off)
- Group list is fetched from the existing `GET /api/v1/groups` endpoint (groups the user is a member of)

### 2. Color assignment per group

- Assign a distinct color from a predefined palette to each group
- Colors should be used consistently:
  - In the filter panel (color swatch next to group name)
  - In the calendar (event blocks colored by their source group)
  - Personal events have their own distinct color
- Colors must work in both light and dark themes
- Colors must meet accessibility contrast requirements
- Use semantic design tokens — no hardcoded color values
- If the user belongs to more groups than the palette supports, cycle through the palette or use a deterministic hash-based assignment

### 3. Filter persistence

- Persist the user's filter selections in local storage
- Restore filter state on page load
- Default: all groups and personal events are shown
- Filter state is per-user (stored locally, not on the server)

### 4. Data fetching behavior

- When a group is toggled off, do NOT fetch its events from the API (optimization)
- When a group is toggled on, fetch events for the visible time range from that group's endpoint
- When filter changes, update the calendar view immediately without full page reload
- Handle the case where a user leaves a group — the group should be removed from the filter list, and its saved filter state cleaned up

### 5. Integration with calendar view

- The filter component integrates with the calendar view from the calendar view UI issue
- Filtered events are merged and displayed in the calendar
- Events from toggled-off groups are not visible in any calendar view mode
- The filter state affects all view modes (day, week, month, custom)

### 6. Mobile considerations

- On mobile, the filter panel should be accessible via a filter icon/button (not always visible)
- When opened, it should display as a sheet or modal
- Filter changes should apply immediately when the panel is closed

---

## Additional notes

- Depends on: Calendar view UI, View group members' shared events, Personal event management
- This is a client-side feature — no new backend API endpoints required
- Uses existing `GET /api/v1/groups` to get the list of groups
- Uses existing `GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId/events` to get events per group
- Uses `GET /api/v1/events` to get personal events
- Color palette should be defined in the theme system alongside existing design tokens
- Consider future extensibility: users may eventually want to filter by event status, visibility level, or other criteria

### Related files
- `client/src/api/groups.ts` — group API client
- `client/src/api/events.ts` — event API client
- `client/src/theme/` — theme system and design tokens
- `client/src/pages/` — calendar page (from calendar view UI issue)
- `client/src/components/` — reusable components

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Group filter panel displays all groups the user belongs to
- [ ] Each group has a toggle to show/hide its events in the calendar
- [ ] "Personal events" toggle to show/hide personal events
- [ ] "Select all" and "Deselect all" controls work
- [ ] Each group has a distinct color indicator
- [ ] Calendar events are color-coded by their source group
- [ ] Filter state persists in local storage across sessions
- [ ] Default state: all groups and personal events are shown
- [ ] Toggling a group off removes its events from the calendar immediately
- [ ] Toggling a group on fetches and displays its events
- [ ] Toggled-off groups' events are not fetched from the API (optimization)
- [ ] Colors work in both light and dark themes
- [ ] Colors meet accessibility contrast requirements
- [ ] Mobile: filter is accessible via icon/button and displays as sheet/modal
- [ ] Filter affects all calendar view modes (day, week, month, custom)
- [ ] Component tests cover filter toggling, persistence, and color assignment
- [ ] Integration tests cover data fetching behavior with filter state
- [ ] CI pipeline passes
